title: Make onboarding a single reproducible command
description: Provide one documented command that installs dependencies, sets up config, and brings the dev environment to a runnable state on a fresh checkout.
body: Use when a new contributor or a fresh machine needs to run the project. Do not document a long manual sequence of undocumented steps, and avoid setup that silently depends on globally-installed tools a newcomer lacks. Actions: encode install, config scaffolding, and any codegen into one entrypoint (make setup, a task runner, or a script), have it check prerequisites and fail with clear guidance, and inspect that it works from a clean clone. Validation signals include a fresh-clone run reaching a runnable state with no manual edits and a documented expected output. Risk boundary: if bootstrap needs a real secret or production credential to complete, stop and require the human to supply it deliberately rather than embedding or defaulting it.
